[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Healthcare | Driver: A Resolution Of South China Sea Tensions

BASE CASE (42% probability): Assumes a resolution of South China Sea tensions unfolds gradually and broadly in line with current consensus expectations. "
Under this path, healthcare is expected to see growth/performance of approximately 4.1% over the next 12 months, "
with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (26% probability): If a resolution of South China Sea tensions materializes faster or more favorably than expected, healthcare could see "
upside of 10% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (32% probability): If a resolution of South China Sea tensions turns more adverse than currently priced in, healthcare could face downside "
of 19% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to healthcare "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 42% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BASE CASE (41% probability): Assumes above-trend global growth unfolds gradually and broadly in line with current consensus expectations. "
Under this path, textiles is expected to see growth/performance of approximately 3.7% over the next 12 months, "
with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (25% probability): If above-trend global growth materializes faster or more favorably than expected, textiles could see "
upside of 10% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (34% probability): If above-trend global growth turns more adverse than currently priced in, textiles could face downside "
of 18% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to textiles "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 41% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Under this path, e-commerce is expected to see growth/performance of approximately 8.4% over the next 12 months, "
with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (29% probability): If a China hard landing materializes faster or more favorably than expected, e-commerce could see "
upside of 6% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (29% probability): If a China hard landing turns more adverse than currently priced in, e-commerce could face downside "
of 19% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to e-commerce "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 42% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (28% probability): If a global trade war escalation materializes faster or more favorably than expected, fintech could see "
upside of 12% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (27% probability): If a global trade war escalation turns more adverse than currently priced in, fintech could face downside "
of 7% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to fintech "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 45% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BULL CASE (19% probability): If a global oil supply shock materializes faster or more favorably than expected, sharia finance could see "
upside of 11% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (41% probability): If a global oil supply shock turns more adverse than currently priced in, sharia finance could face downside "
of 24% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to sharia finance "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 40% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BULL CASE (28% probability): If a global recession materializes faster or more favorably than expected, the nickel market could see "
upside of 9% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (12% probability): If a global recession turns more adverse than currently priced in, the nickel market could face downside "
of 11% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the nickel market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 60% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (25% probability): If a China hard landing materializes faster or more favorably than expected, the palm oil market could see "
upside of 19% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (20% probability): If a China hard landing turns more adverse than currently priced in, the palm oil market could face downside "
of 9% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the palm oil market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 55% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (29% probability): If an Indonesian political transition materializes faster or more favorably than expected, the nickel market could see "
upside of 14% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (13% probability): If an Indonesian political transition turns more adverse than currently priced in, the nickel market could face downside "
of 8% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the nickel market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 58% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (24% probability): If a China stimulus surge materializes faster or more favorably than expected, aviation could see "
upside of 14% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (16% probability): If a China stimulus surge turns more adverse than currently priced in, aviation could face downside "
of 23% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to aviation "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 60% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

with volatility remaining within historical norms. Policy responses are expected to be measured and reactive rather than aggressive.

BULL CASE (16% probability): If a hawkish Fed surprise materializes faster or more favorably than expected, fintech could see "
upside of 15% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (43% probability): If a hawkish Fed surprise turns more adverse than currently priced in, fintech could face downside "
of 23% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to fintech "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 41% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BULL CASE (25% probability): If a global recession materializes faster or more favorably than expected, healthcare could see "
upside of 15% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (29% probability): If a global recession turns more adverse than currently priced in, healthcare could face downside "
of 8% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to healthcare "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 46% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (40% probability): If a China stimulus surge turns more adverse than currently priced in, the palm oil market could face downside "
of 5% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the palm oil market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 44% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

upside of 21% relative to the base case, driven by improved sentiment, capital inflows, and a re-rating of risk premia. "
Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (15% probability): If a commodity supercycle turns more adverse than currently priced in, technology could face downside "
of 21% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to technology "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 60% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (37% probability): If above-trend global growth turns more adverse than currently priced in, nickel mining could face downside "
of 17% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to nickel mining "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 40% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (22% probability): If an Indonesian sovereign rating upgrade turns more adverse than currently priced in, banking could face downside "
of 11% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to banking "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 53% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (26% probability): If an Indonesian sovereign rating upgrade turns more adverse than currently priced in, the nickel market could face downside "
of 12% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the nickel market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 53% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BEAR CASE (21% probability): If a hawkish Fed surprise turns more adverse than currently priced in, Indonesian banking system liquidity could face downside "
of 21% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to Indonesian banking system liquidity "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 55% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

of 18% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to healthcare "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 47% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BEAR CASE (28% probability): If an Indonesian political transition turns more adverse than currently priced in, retail could face downside "
of 10% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to retail "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 54% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

of 18% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to e-commerce "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 46% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to nickel mining "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 52% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 41% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to pharmaceuticals "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 51% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BEAR CASE (29% probability): If a sharp dollar depreciation turns more adverse than currently priced in, the palm oil market could face downside "
of 25% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the palm oil market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 53% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (28% probability): If an escalation in Middle East tensions turns more adverse than currently priced in, tourism could face downside "
of 7% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to tourism "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 47% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (24% probability): If above-trend global growth turns more adverse than currently priced in, mining services could face downside "
of 8% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to mining services "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 46% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

Key catalysts to watch: faster-than-expected policy easing, stronger capital inflows, and positive surprises in underlying fundamentals.

BEAR CASE (21% probability): If a Fed pivot to rate cuts turns more adverse than currently priced in, the palm oil market could face downside "
of 8% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to the palm oil market "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 58% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

BEAR CASE (28% probability): If a resolution of South China Sea tensions turns more adverse than currently priced in, fintech could face downside "
of 10% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to fintech "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 55% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

of 22% relative to the base case, as risk aversion rises, capital flows reverse, and credit conditions tighten. "
Key risk triggers: policy missteps, contagion from external shocks, and a sharper-than-expected deterioration in fundamentals.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Investors with high conviction in the bull case should consider increasing exposure to infrastructure "
via direct allocation or related derivatives, while those concerned about the bear case may consider hedges, reduced position "
sizing, or rotation into defensive alternatives. Given the 59% base-case weighting, a barbell approach (core base-case "
exposure plus tail-risk hedges) is generally favored over a binary bet on either extreme.

# ===== EXPANDED SCENARIO PLAYBOOKS =====

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Global Equities | Driver: A US Recession

BASE CASE (38% probability): Assumes a US recession unfolds as a mild contraction (-0.5% GDP for two quarters) triggered by overtightening. Under this path, global equities are expected to see a drawdown of approximately -15% over the next 12 months, with the S&P 500 falling to 4,700 before recovering. Earnings contraction of 10-12% is expected, concentrated in cyclical sectors. EM equities initially sell off on risk aversion but recover faster as the Fed pivots to cuts.

BULL CASE (22% probability): If the US avoids recession entirely (soft landing achieved), global equities could see upside of 12% relative to the base case, driven by earnings resilience and a Fed easing cycle boosting risk appetite. Key catalysts to watch: inflation returning to 2.5%, labor market remaining above 3.5% unemployment, and consumer spending maintaining 2%+ growth. EM equities, including Indonesian stocks, would benefit from a weaker dollar and capital rotation into higher-yielding markets.

BEAR CASE (40% probability): If the recession deepens into a severe contraction (-2% GDP) with financial system stress (CRE defaults, regional bank failures), global equities could face downside of 30% relative to the base case. Credit spreads would widen 300+ bps, VIX would spike to 40+, and foreign investors would flee EM markets aggressively. The Indonesian JCI could test 6,000 support, with banking stocks bearing the brunt of foreign selling.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The elevated bear-case probability (40%) warrants defensive positioning. Investors should reduce exposure to US cyclical stocks and high-beta EM equities, increase allocation to investment-grade bonds and gold, and build cash reserves for opportunistic buying during the drawdown. A 60% defensive / 40% cash-plus-gold barbell is favored over concentrated equity positions.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Indonesian Banking | Driver: BI Rate Cut Cycle

BASE CASE (45% probability): Assumes BI begins a gradual easing cycle in Q4 2026 with 25 bps cuts, reaching 5.25% by mid-2027. Under this path, Indonesian banking is expected to see mixed performance: NIM compression of 30-50 bps offsets volume growth of 12-15% in credit. Banking stocks deliver total returns of 10-12% (6-8% dividends + modest capital appreciation). BBCA outperforms due to high CASA protecting its funding cost, while smaller banks with higher time deposit ratios underperform.

BULL CASE (25% probability): If BI cuts aggressively (100 bps in 6 months) due to a sharp global slowdown forcing preemptive easing, banking stocks could see upside of 20% relative to the base case. Credit growth would accelerate to 15-18%, property lending would revive, and the JCI would receive $2-3B in foreign inflows. BBRI would be the primary beneficiary given its micro-lending franchise that is most rate-sensitive.

BEAR CASE (30% probability): If BI is forced to hike rates to 6.25% to defend the Rupiah (triggered by a Fed hawkish surprise or oil price spike), banking stocks could face downside of 15% relative to the base case. Credit growth would slow to 6-8%, NPLs would rise from 2.2% to 3.0%, and dividend payout ratios could be cut. The most vulnerable banks would be those with high CRE exposure and thin provision coverage ratios below 150%.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The asymmetric risk (bull and base cases combined = 70% probability of flat-to-positive returns) supports maintaining exposure to Indonesian banks, with a preference for BBCA (quality, NIM resilience) and BBRI (yield, rate-cut beneficiary). Underweight smaller banks with weaker CASA ratios. Use put options on the banking index as tail-risk hedges.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Bitcoin and Crypto | Driver: US Spot ETF Flow Dynamics

BASE CASE (40% probability): Assumes ETF inflows moderate from $200M daily to $100M daily as initial pent-up demand is absorbed. Under this path, Bitcoin is expected to trade in a $65,000-$80,000 range over the next 12 months, with volatility declining to annualized 40% (from 55% historically). Ethereum maintains $3,200-$4,500 range. DeFi TVL grows to $130B. Regulatory clarity continues incrementally.

BULL CASE (30% probability): If ETF inflows accelerate due to institutional allocations (pension funds, endowments adding 1-3% crypto allocation), Bitcoin could reach $100,000-$120,000, driven by a supply-demand imbalance. Catalysts: Fed rate cuts driving risk-on rotation, a major sovereign wealth fund announcing BTC allocation, or spot Solana ETF approval. Ethereum could reach $6,000 on L2 adoption and staking yield demand. Altcoins would rally 2-3x BTC performance.

BEAR CASE (30% probability): If regulatory crackdowns intensify (SEC enforcement actions against DeFi protocols, Tether regulatory challenge, or a major exchange failure), Bitcoin could retest $45,000-$50,000, with altcoins falling 50-70%. ETF outflows would accelerate as institutional investors face compliance pressure. The DeFi ecosystem would see a repeat of the 2022 deleveraging cascade, with restaking protocols particularly vulnerable.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The balanced probability distribution favors a core BTC/ETH position (70% of crypto allocation) with tactical altcoin exposure (30%) for upside capture. DCA into BTC over 6-12 months rather than lump-sum entry. Maintain stablecoin reserves (10-15% of crypto portfolio) for opportunistic buying during corrections. Avoid leveraged positions given 30% bear-case probability.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Copper and Base Metals | Driver: Green Transition Demand Acceleration

BASE CASE (45% probability): Assumes copper supply deficit persists at 500,000 tons but is partially offset by recycling and substitution. Under this path, copper is expected to trade at $10,500-$11,500/ton over the next 12 months. Indonesian nickel prices stabilize at $18,000-$20,000/ton as environmental audits conclude and production resumes. Base metal equities deliver 8-12% total returns.

BULL CASE (30% probability): If green transition spending accelerates (US IRA expansion, EU Green Deal Phase 2, China grid investment), copper could reach $12,000-$14,000/ton as the supply deficit widens to 800,000 tons. Nickel benefits from EV battery demand outpacing stainless steel headwinds. Indonesian mining stocks (Antam, Vale Indonesia) would re-rate 20-30%, and the government could impose higher royalty rates to capture windfall.

BEAR CASE (25% probability): If a global recession slashes industrial demand by 10%, base metal prices could fall 20-25% from current levels. Copper to $8,000, nickel to $15,000. Mine development projects would be delayed, paradoxically worsening the long-term supply deficit but destroying near-term equity value. Indonesian coal prices would also fall, creating fiscal pressure.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The structural bull case for copper (25-year electrification supercycle) supports long-term allocation despite short-term recession risk. Prefer copper miners with low-cost operations and long reserve lives. For nickel, Indonesian producers offer the best risk-reward due to low extraction costs and proximity to Chinese demand. Hedge cyclical risk with gold exposure.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Palm Oil | Driver: EUDR Implementation and Biodiesel Mandate

BASE CASE (50% probability): Assumes EUDR implementation proceeds in 2027 with partial compliance from Indonesian producers (60% of large plantations, 30% of smallholders). CPO prices trade at $3,800-$4,200/ton. B40 biodiesel mandate absorbs 5M additional tons domestically. Net export volumes decline 8-10% but higher prices maintain revenue.

BULL CASE (20% probability): If EUDR is further delayed or significantly softened (recognizing ISPO as equivalent to EU standards), Indonesian palm oil regains full EU market access. Combined with B40 demand, CPO could reach $4,800-$5,200/ton. Palm oil plantation stocks (AALI, LSIP) would re-rate 25-30%. Smallholder incomes improve significantly.

BEAR CASE (30% probability): If EUDR is strictly enforced with limited ISPO recognition, 40% of Indonesian palm oil exports to the EU could be blocked. Prices would bifurcate: compliant CPO at $4,500/ton for EU, non-compliant CPO at $3,200/ton for India and China. Smallholder income drops 20%, creating social and political pressure. Government may be forced to increase subsidy spending.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The base case favors large plantation companies with advanced traceability systems (Sinar Mas, Wilmar) over smallholder-dependent producers. The B40 mandate provides a domestic demand floor regardless of EUDR outcome, limiting downside for the sector as a whole. Pair long palm oil exposure with short soybean oil as a relative value trade, given palm oil's cost advantage.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Indonesian Rupiah | Driver: Fed Policy Divergence

BASE CASE (45% probability): Assumes the Fed delivers one 25 bps cut in December 2026, and BI holds at 5.75% until the cut materializes. USD/IDR trades in a 16,000-16,400 range with BI smoothing volatility through DNDF intervention. FX reserves remain adequate above $140B. Carry trade flows support the Rupiah within this band.

BULL CASE (25% probability): If the Fed cuts earlier or more aggressively than expected (50 bps in Q3), USD weakens broadly, and the Rupiah appreciates to 15,500-15,800. Foreign bond inflows surge $3-5B as the yield spread widens. BI can cut rates by 50 bps without currency pressure. This is the best-case scenario for Indonesian assets across the board — equities, bonds, and currency all rally simultaneously.

BEAR CASE (30% probability): If the Fed delays cuts to 2027 or delivers a hawkish surprise, USD/IDR could break 16,500-17,000. BI would be forced to hike rates to 6.0-6.25% to defend the currency, choking domestic credit growth. Foreign investors would accelerate bond and equity selling. The carry trade would reverse, with $2-3B in outflows from IDR bonds. This scenario most closely resembles the 2013 taper tantrum, though Indonesia's fundamentals are stronger now.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The asymmetric payoff favors maintaining a structurally long IDR position (via Indonesian bonds yielding 6.85%) with tail-risk hedges via 3-month USD/IDR call options struck at 16,500. The option premium of 1.5-2.0% annually is a reasonable insurance cost against the bear case. For equity investors, overweight exporters (coal, palm oil, textiles) that benefit from Rupiah weakness in the bear case, providing a natural hedge.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Global Trade | Driver: US-China Decoupling Acceleration

BASE CASE (40% probability): Assumes US-China tensions remain elevated but contained, with selective tariffs and export controls. Global trade volume grows 1-2%, below the 3% historical average. ASEAN benefits from China Plus One diversification, with Indonesia's manufacturing FDI growing 10-15% annually. Supply chain restructuring is gradual (5-10 year process).

BULL CASE (20% probability): If a US-China diplomatic breakthrough occurs (e.g., trade deal Phase 3), global trade volumes recover to 3%+ growth. Commodity prices rally on improved demand outlook. Indonesia benefits from both Chinese demand recovery (exports) and continued diversification investment (FDI). JCI could rally 10-15% on improved global sentiment.

BEAR CASE (40% probability): If decoupling accelerates (secondary sanctions on Chinese tech companies, Taiwan contingency escalation, or BRICS-G7 financial fragmentation), global trade volume could contract 2-3%. Indonesia faces a difficult choice between its Chinese trade relationships ($50B annually) and US strategic partnership. Commodity prices would diverge — oil spikes on supply disruption, while industrial metals fall on demand destruction. The JCI would face significant foreign selling pressure.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The elevated bear-case probability (40%) warrants geographic diversification and reduced exposure to global trade-sensitive sectors. Favor domestic consumption plays (healthcare, telecommunications, consumer staples) over export-dependent sectors (mining, textiles, palm oil). Maintain gold at 10-15% of portfolio as a geopolitical hedge. Consider Indonesia's BRICS membership as a potential buffer — access to alternative trade financing channels may partially offset G7 sanctions risk.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: DeFi and Tokenization | Driver: Institutional Adoption Wave

BASE CASE (42% probability): Assumes gradual institutional adoption with RWA tokenization growing to $25B TVL by mid-2027. DeFi total TVL grows to $150B. Regulatory frameworks (MiCA, OJK sandbox) provide clarity but limit innovation. Staking yields compress to 3.0-3.5% as capital floods in. Major banks launch tokenized deposit products.

BULL CASE (28% probability): If a G7 central bank announces a CBDC interoperable with DeFi protocols, or if a major asset manager (BlackRock, Vanguard) tokenizes a $50B+ fund, DeFi TVL could reach $300B. RWA tokenization would cross $50B, creating a parallel financial system. DeFi tokens (AAVE, UNI, MKR) would re-rate 3-5x. Indonesia's OJK sandbox could accelerate nickel and bond tokenization.

BEAR CASE (30% probability): If a major DeFi protocol fails (restaking cascade, stablecoin depeg, or oracle manipulation causing $5B+ in losses), institutional confidence would collapse. Regulators would respond with restrictive legislation, potentially banning non-compliant DeFi in major jurisdictions. TVL could fall 50% to $60B. The narrative of DeFi as institutional infrastructure would be set back 3-5 years.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The risk-reward favors selective exposure to DeFi infrastructure tokens (AAVE, LINK) and RWA platforms over speculative DeFi protocols. Avoid concentrated exposure to restaking (EigenLayer) given contagion risk in the bear case. For Indonesian investors, the OJK sandbox represents a unique opportunity to access tokenized bonds and commodities — position early with small allocations (2-5% of portfolio) and scale up as regulatory clarity improves.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Indonesian Consumer Sector | Driver: VAT Hike and Consumer Sentiment

BASE CASE (48% probability): Assumes the VAT hike from 11% to 12% proceeds as planned in January 2027, with government offsetting measures (expanded social protection) partially cushioning the impact. Consumer spending growth slows from 4.8% to 3.5%. Consumer staples outperform as households trade down; consumer discretionary underperforms as big-ticket purchases are deferred. Retail sales growth moderates to 5-7%.

BULL CASE (18% probability): If the government delays or cancels the VAT hike (due to political pressure or stronger-than-expected fiscal revenue from commodities), consumer confidence would rebound sharply. Pent-up demand would drive 6-8% consumption growth. Consumer discretionary, automotive, and property sectors would rally 15-20%. E-commerce GMV would reaccelerate to 20% growth.

BEAR CASE (34% probability): If the VAT hike coincides with Rupiah depreciation above 16,500 (raising imported goods prices) and a global commodity downturn (reducing household income in resource-dependent regions), consumer spending could contract in real terms. Savings rate would fall below 3%, credit card delinquencies would rise, and consumer-facing companies would face 10-15% earnings downgrades. FMCG companies would cut margins to maintain volume, while luxury and premium segments would see 20%+ sales declines.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: The high base-case probability (48%) and significant bear-case tail (34%) warrant a defensive consumer portfolio. Overweight consumer staples (Indofood, Unilever) and discount retailers that benefit from trading-down behavior. Underweight automotive, property, and luxury retail. Within e-commerce, favor platforms with strong unit economics (Tokopedia) over growth-at-all-cost players. Healthcare (pharmaceuticals, hospitals) acts as a consumer sub-sector with defensive characteristics and secular growth from aging demographics.

[SCENARIO PLAYBOOK] Subject: Gold and Precious Metals | Driver: De-Dollarization and Central Bank Buying

BASE CASE (40% probability): Assumes central bank gold purchases remain elevated at 1,000-1,200 tons annually and the Fed delivers one cut in late 2026. Gold trades in a $2,350-$2,550/oz range. Silver benefits from both industrial demand (solar panels) and monetary demand, trading at $28-$33. Gold miners generate 8-10% FCF yields and continue dividend increases.

BULL CASE (30% probability): If de-dollarization accelerates (BRICS payment system expansion, Saudi Arabia pricing oil partially in yuan, or a major geopolitical escalation), gold could reach $2,800-$3,000/oz as central banks and retail investors pile into the safe haven. Silver would outperform with a gold/silver ratio compressing from 82 to 70, implying $38-$40 silver. Gold miners would re-rate from 8x to 12x P/E, delivering 50%+ returns.

BEAR CASE (30% probability): If a diplomatic breakthrough reduces geopolitical tensions and the Fed maintains a hawkish stance (no cuts in 2026), gold could correct to $2,100-$2,200 as the geopolitical premium evaporates and real yields rise. Central bank buying would moderate. Gold miners would underperform, with the sector facing 20% downside from current levels. Silver would fall proportionally more due to industrial demand weakness.

POSITIONING IMPLICATIONS: Gold at $2,455 is fairly valued in the base case but has significant optionality in the bull case. Maintain a core gold position of 10% of portfolio, split between physical/ETF (60%) and gold miners (40%). Gold miners offer leverage — a 15% gold price increase translates to 30-40% earnings increase for low-cost producers. Consider adding silver exposure for portfolio convexity, as the gold/silver ratio at 82 is historically elevated and mean-reverts to 65-70 over time.
